data.title: Tidewater (TDW) Q4 Margin Strength Challenges Bearish Earnings Decline Narrative
data.description:
Advertisement

Tidewater FY 2025 earnings snapshot

Tidewater (TDW) closed FY 2025 with Q4 revenue of US$336.8 million and basic EPS of US$4.44, with trailing twelve month revenue at about US$1.4 billion and EPS of US$6.69 setting the backdrop for this earnings season. The company reported quarterly revenue in the US$333 million to US$341 million range over FY 2025. Basic EPS moved from US$0.83 in Q1 to US$1.47 in Q2, then a small loss in Q3 and US$4.44 in Q4. Together with a higher trailing net margin, this highlights earnings power that investors will now assess for consistency and quality.

See our full analysis for Tidewater.

With the latest numbers on the table, the key question is how they compare with widely held views on Tidewater's growth, profitability and risks, and where those narratives might need to be updated.

See what the community is saying about Tidewater

Margin strength behind US$335 million Q4 profit

  • On a trailing basis, Tidewater generated US$334.7 million of net income on US$1.35b of revenue, which lines up with a 24.7% net margin compared with 13.4% a year earlier.
  • What stands out for the bullish camp is how this margin story interacts with their view that profit margins could move toward the low 20s over the next few years, yet:
    • Trailing EPS of US$6.69 and net income of US$334.7 million already sit above the US$198.9 million earnings figure used in the forward narratives, so bulls need to square that with their margin expansion argument.
    • At the same time, three consecutive quarters of roughly US$333 million to US$341 million revenue with very different EPS outcomes shows margins are sensitive to items below the revenue line. This challenges simple bullish claims that higher margins automatically translate into smooth EPS growth.
Over the last year, bulls have argued that tight vessel supply and stronger offshore activity can support higher day rates and margins. This margin profile is a key part of that debate, and the full bull case sets out how they see it playing out over time. 🐂 Tidewater Bull Case

Share price, P/E and DCF gap

  • With a share price of US$87.67 and trailing EPS of US$6.69, Tidewater trades on a P/E of about 13x, while the supplied DCF fair value is US$211.35, creating a wide gap between price and that reference value.
  • Critics watching the bearish narrative point out that even with this 13x P/E and a price well below the DCF fair value, there are questions about how long the current earnings level can last:
    • The analysis data indicates earnings are forecast to decline by about 9.1% per year over the next three years, which sits uncomfortably next to a DCF fair value that is more than double the current share price.
    • Bears also flag that revenue is expected to grow around 6.1% per year compared with a 10.2% forecast for the broader US market, so they see slower growth and declining earnings as key reasons why a valuation gap could persist even with a lower P/E than industry and peers.
Skeptics often focus on this mix of a low P/E, a large DCF fair value gap and a projected earnings decline. The bear case lays out how that could influence the multiple investors are willing to pay over time. 🐻 Tidewater Bear Case

Earnings path: strong trailing growth vs decline forecast

  • Trailing earnings growth over the past year is cited at 85.2%, with the past five years averaging 71.7% per year, yet the same dataset flags that earnings are expected to decline by about 9.1% per year over the next three years.
  • Analysts' consensus-style narrative sits between the bull and bear cases and has to balance that sharp trailing growth with more muted forward expectations:
    • On one hand, improved profitability and a 24.7% net margin give some support to the idea that Tidewater has moved into a stronger phase of its business cycle.
    • On the other, the forecast of slower revenue growth than the wider US market and declining earnings means this strong historical run is not automatically treated as a guide to what comes next. This is why the provided consensus price target of US$74.67 is closer to the current price than the DCF fair value figure.

Approach 1: New Oriental Education & Technology Group Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimated future cash flows, then discounts them back into today’s dollars to arrive at an implied value per share. It is essentially asking what those future cash flows are worth right now.

For New Oriental Education & Technology Group, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $656.4 million, and analysts plus extrapolations project annual free cash flows such as $682.6 million in 2026 and $734.3 million in 2027, with further projections extended out to 2035 by Simply Wall St based on the supplied path of estimates.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted back, the resulting estimated intrinsic value comes out at about $67.61 per share. Against the recent share price of $52.55, the model implies the stock trades at roughly a 22.3% discount, which indicates potential undervaluation on this specific cash flow view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: New Oriental Education & Technology Group Price vs Earnings

For a profitable business like New Oriental Education & Technology Group, the P/E ratio is a useful yardstick because it links what you pay per share to the earnings the company is already generating. It helps you see how many dollars of price the market is paying for each dollar of current earnings.

What counts as a “fair” P/E depends on what investors expect for future growth and how much risk they see in those earnings. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth and higher risk usually point to a lower multiple.

New Oriental currently trades on a P/E of 21.98x, compared with a Consumer Services industry average of about 18.23x and a peer group average of 18.12x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for New Oriental is 27.39x, which is its proprietary estimate of a P/E that reflects the company’s earnings growth profile, profit margins, risk characteristics, industry and market cap. This Fair Ratio can be more informative than simple peer or industry comparisons because it adjusts for those company specific factors. Since the Fair Ratio sits above the current 21.98x P/E, this framework suggests the shares could be pricing in a lower multiple than those fundamentals might support.

data.title: Guardant Health Targets Early Detection Growth With New Partner And Acquisition
data.description:

  • The company also agreed to acquire MetaSight Diagnostics, adding next generation liquid biopsy technology aimed at early disease detection.

Guardant Health, a precision oncology company listed on NasdaqGS:GH, focuses on blood based tests for cancer screening, treatment selection and disease monitoring. The collaboration with Patrick Dempsey brings additional attention to colorectal cancer screening options, including Guardant Health’s Shield blood test. Early detection remains a major health priority, and higher public awareness can be an important ingredient for test adoption over time.

The MetaSight Diagnostics acquisition adds to Guardant Health’s toolkit for earlier and more refined detection of disease using liquid biopsy approaches. As the company integrates MetaSight’s technology and research pipeline, investors may monitor how Guardant Health positions its expanded testing portfolio across screening, diagnostics and treatment decision support in the coming years.

Tradeweb Markets scores just 0/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Tradeweb Markets Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a company can earn over and above the return that equity investors require, then capitalizes those extra profits into a per share value. It focuses less on short term earnings swings and more on the long run return on the equity base.

For Tradeweb Markets, the model starts with a Book Value of US$30.63 per share and a Stable EPS estimate of US$4.50 per share, based on weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 6 analysts. The implied Cost of Equity is US$2.84 per share, which leaves an Excess Return of US$1.66 per share. That sits on an Average Return on Equity assumption of 12.32% and a Stable Book Value of US$36.55 per share, sourced from weighted future Book Value estimates from 3 analysts.

When these excess returns are projected and discounted, the model arrives at an intrinsic value of US$74.49 per share. Against the recent share price of US$125.61, this suggests the stock is around 68.6% overvalued on this framework.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Excess Returns analysis suggests Tradeweb Markets may be overvalued by 68.6%. Discover 49 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Tradeweb Markets.

Approach 2: Tradeweb Markets Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like Tradeweb Markets, the P/E ratio is a useful way to link what you pay today to the earnings the business is generating. It gives you a quick sense of how many dollars of share price investors are willing to pay for each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends a lot on growth expectations and risk. Higher expected earnings growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually line up with a lower P/E. So context really matters when you look at a single number.

Tradeweb Markets currently trades on a P/E of 32.88x. That sits slightly above the peer average of 32.42x and well above the wider Capital Markets industry average of 22.70x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 16.25x, which reflects factors such as the company’s earnings growth profile, its industry, profit margins, market cap and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is designed to give you a more tailored anchor than a simple peer or industry comparison, because it adjusts for quality and risk rather than assuming all Capital Markets stocks should trade on similar multiples. Setting the current 32.88x P/E against the 16.25x Fair Ratio points to Tradeweb Markets trading above that fair range.

Result: OVERVALUED

🐻 Tradeweb Markets Bear Case

data.title: Philip Morris Donation Highlights Community Ties And Long Term Investor Story
data.description:
• Philip Morris International's U.S. arm has committed a significant donation to the California Asian Pacific Chamber of Commerce.
  • The funding is aimed at supporting small business growth initiatives, especially for socially and economically disadvantaged entrepreneurs.
  • The move reflects the company’s wider focus on social impact and business ecosystem development beyond its core tobacco operations.

For investors following Philip Morris International, NYSE:PM, this initiative sits alongside a current share price of $177.93 and a 1 year return of 19.7%. The stock has also seen a 5 year return of 160.3%, which indicates how the market has historically valued the business while it invests in broader community partnerships.

While this donation does not change the core tobacco exposure of NYSE:PM, it shows the company engaging more directly with small business communities in the U.S. For investors, it is another data point to monitor as the company builds relationships that could influence brand perception and stakeholder discussions over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Philip Morris International by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Philip Morris International.

We've flagged 2 risks for Philip Morris International. See which could impact your investment.

For you as an investor, the CalAsian Chamber donation is a relatively small capital outlay next to Philip Morris International's reaffirmed 2026 EPS guidance of US$7.87 to US$8.02, but it sits in an important context. PMI is working to reposition itself around smoke free products while still operating in a heavily scrutinised industry. Supporting small business programs in California can help PMI U.S. build relationships with local entrepreneurs, policymakers and community groups, which may matter when issues such as product access, zoning or regulation come up. It also gives the company another proof point when it talks about economic contribution and inclusion, at a time when tobacco peers like Altria and British American Tobacco are under similar scrutiny. On its own, this donation does not change the earnings outlook or the business mix between combustibles and smoke free products, and it does not address core risks such as regulation, litigation or illicit trade. It does, however, show the company continuing to invest in ecosystem building alongside its commercial focus on IQOS, ZYN and other reduced risk products.

How This Fits Into The Philip Morris International Narrative

  • The focus on supporting socially and economically disadvantaged entrepreneurs lines up with PMI's broader push to present itself as a long term partner in communities as it shifts toward smoke free products.
  • The initiative does not directly address narrative risks such as regulatory pressure on nicotine products or a possible slowdown in smoke free growth, so those concerns remain in play.
  • Community partnership activity of this kind is not a major feature of the existing narrative, so investors may want to consider whether repeated programs like this could influence brand perception and stakeholder relationships over time.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged ongoing risks around high debt levels, which this kind of community spending does not reduce.
  • ⚠️ The donation does not change core industry risks such as regulation, taxation or declining cigarette volumes, which can still affect revenue and margins.
  • 🎁 PMI is viewed as having a good financial position, which gives it room to fund community programs alongside its smoke free investments.
  • 🎁 The company has been growing profit or revenue according to analyst commentary, and community initiatives may support its positioning as it scales IQOS, ZYN and other reduced risk products.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to watch whether PMI continues to expand this type of partnership in other U.S. regions, and if management starts to link these programs more explicitly to its smoke free strategy on earnings calls or in guidance updates. The reaffirmed 2026 EPS range gives a reference point for how much non core spending the business is comfortable absorbing. It is also worth tracking how competitors like Altria, British American Tobacco and Japan Tobacco handle their own community and small business engagement, to see if PMI is moving ahead of or simply in line with the sector. Any shift in regulatory tone toward nicotine pouches or heated products in key markets will likely matter more to the investment case than the size of this donation, but these community ties could influence those discussions at the margin.

data.title: Is ISS (CPSE:ISS) Offering A Fresh Opportunity After Recent Share Price Pullback
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether ISS shares still offer value at around kr218.20, you are not alone. Many investors are asking if the current price fairly reflects the business.
  • The stock has had a mixed run, with a 3.4% decline over the last 7 days and an 11.0% decline over the last 30 days, while the 1 year return sits at 39.2% and the 5 year return at 87.9%.
  • Recent news around ISS has focused on its role as a large facilities services provider and how it is positioning itself with customers and contracts. This helps frame how investors think about its future cash generation. This context is important when you look at the recent share price moves and try to judge whether sentiment has run ahead of fundamentals or is still catching up.
  • On our valuation checks ISS scores 4 out of 6 for being undervalued, and you can see the breakdown of that valuation score. Next we will walk through the standard valuation approaches behind that score and then finish with a broader way to think about what valuation really means for your own process.

ISS delivered 39.2% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Commercial Services industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: ISS Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For ISS, the latest twelve month free cash flow (FCF) is reported at DKK 3.30b. Analysts have provided explicit FCF estimates for the next few years, and Simply Wall St then extrapolates those out to build a 10 year path. By 2035, the model is using an FCF projection of DKK 4.21b, with interim years such as 2028 using DKK 3.61b based on analyst inputs.

Using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach and discounting all those DKK cash flows back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of DKK 565.11 per share. Compared with the recent share price of about DKK 218.20, the DCF suggests ISS is trading at a 61.4% discount to this intrinsic estimate. On this measure alone, the stock appears materially undervalued.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests ISS is undervalued by 61.4%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 230 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for ISS.

Approach 2: ISS Price vs Earnings

The P/E ratio is a useful way to look at profitable companies because it directly links what you pay for each share to the earnings that company is currently generating. It gives you a quick sense of how many years of current earnings you are paying for at today’s price.

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth potential and risk. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk tend to support higher P/E ratios, while lower growth expectations or higher risk usually point to a lower P/E.

ISS currently trades on a P/E of 13.33x. That sits below the Commercial Services industry average P/E of 17.48x and also below the peer group average of 24.70x. Simply Wall St uses a proprietary “Fair Ratio” to refine this comparison. The Fair Ratio is the P/E that would be expected for ISS given factors such as its earnings growth profile, profit margins, industry, market cap and specific risks. This tends to be more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or an industry average because it adjusts for company specific characteristics.

Simply Wall St does not report a Fair Ratio for ISS at the moment. On this approach the stock looks cheaper than both its industry and peers, but there is no precise Fair Ratio comparison available.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 96 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your ISS Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, and on Simply Wall St this comes through Narratives. You and other investors connect a clear story about ISS to specific numbers for future revenue, earnings and margins, link that forecast to a Fair Value, and then compare it with the current price to help decide whether ISS looks attractive or stretched. Each Narrative lives on the Community page and updates automatically as new news or earnings arrive. One investor might see ISS as worth DKK 250.0 per share based on revenue of DKK 96.4b, earnings of DKK 3.3b and a P/E of 13.5x by 2029, while another might see it as worth DKK 150.0 on revenue of DKK 91.8b, earnings of DKK 3.1b and a P/E of 8.5x by 2028. You can then quickly see which story and Fair Value line up best with your own view.

For ISS however we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading ISS Narratives:

These sit at different points on the range of analyst expectations and give you a clear sense of what you would need to believe about revenue, margins and valuation to line up with each side.

🐂 ISS Bull Case

Fair value in this narrative: DKK 250.0

Implied discount to this fair value versus the last close of DKK 218.20: about 13%

Revenue growth assumption: 4.41% per year

  • Assumes ISS grows revenue to around DKK 96.4b by 2029, with profit margins edging up from 3.2% to 3.4%.
  • Sees earnings reaching DKK 3.3b, or DKK 23.86 per share, supported by large contracts, expansion with key accounts and a more scalable U.S. platform.
  • Requires the shares to trade on a P/E of 13.5x in 2029, roughly in line with current levels and similar to the Commercial Services industry P/E used in the narrative.

🐻 ISS Bear Case

Fair value in this narrative: DKK 210.2

Implied premium to this fair value versus the last close of DKK 218.20: about 4%

Revenue growth assumption: 3.42% per year

  • Assumes revenue reaches about DKK 93.7b by 2028 and earnings of DKK 3.2b, with margins moving from 3.2% to 3.5%.
  • Builds in a lower future P/E of 11.5x on those 2028 earnings, compared with a higher multiple used today in the narrative, which keeps the fair value closer to the current price.
  • Views the current share price and the DKK 210.2 consensus target as being fairly close, so the shares sit near what this group of analysts sees as a reasonable estimate of value.

Together these two Narratives frame a range of outcomes from around DKK 210.2 to DKK 250.0 per share. Your next step is to decide which set of assumptions about growth, margins and the appropriate P/E feels more realistic for ISS, and where your own view of fair value sits within that range.
